Notice Of Motion And Motion For Attorneys Fees Pursuant To California Code Of Civil Procedure Section 425.16(C)
Off Calendar Oct-13-2025 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Bryan Kirsh, Esq., SHEGERIAN & ASSOCIATES, INC. (310) 860 0770 BKirsh@Shegerianlaw.com Attorney for Plaintiff, DON LEMON
Ari Holtzblatt, Esq., WILMER CUTLER PICKERING HALE AND DORR LLP (202) 663-6000 ari.holtzblatt@wilmerhale.com Attorney for Defendant X CORP
Heather Pitvoec, CSR #10551 808-446-4538 hpreporting@gmail.com Court reporter,.

Hearing is held. ARGUED. The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANT X CORP.'s Motion For Attorneys Fees Pursuant To California Code Of Civil Procedure Section 425.16(C).

Defendant X Corp.'s motion for anti-SLAPP motion attorneys' fees is granted in part. X Corp. is awarded fees of $150,000 against plaintiff Don Lemon. X Corp. is entitled to an award of fees for partially prevailing on its anti-SLAPP motion. The amount sought by X Corp. is excessive due to the relatively modest practical effect in favor of X Corp. of striking the claims that were stricken and the relative simplicity and lack of novelty of the issues on the stricken claims. Using its own experience and judgment based on adjudicating numerous anti-SLAPP motions, the court finds that 200 hours of attorney time is the maximum reasonable amount of time highly compensated counsel reasonably needed to spend on the portions of the anti-SLAPP motion that X Corp. prevailed on. The court calculated a lodestar of $200,000 based on a blended hourly rate of $1000, and reduced that amount by a negative multiplier of .25 to account for the modest practical effect of striking the claims that were stricken.

Proposed order submitted.

Visiting Judge: Harold E. Kahn; Clerk: W. Trupek; Court Reporter: Heather Pitvorec, CSR #10551 808-446-4538 hpreporting@gmail.com, Reported (301/HEK)

DEFENDANT X CORP.'s Motion To Seal.

Defendant X Corp.'s motion to seal Exhibit B to the Declaration of Ari Holtzblatt in Support of X Corp.'s Motion for Anti-SLAPP attorneys' fees is denied. X Corp. fails to present evidence showing that there is an overriding interest in protecting the privacy of its attorneys' hourly rates for this case or that, even if there was such an overriding interest, that the overriding interest would be prejudiced if this motion is not granted.
